channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Serbian capital braces for a mega pro-president rally as tensions surge in the Balkan country
item.description: Chanting patriotic songs, thousands of supporters of Serbia’s populist President Aleksandar Vucic gathered in downtown Belgrade, a day ahead of what authorities expect to be a huge rally in his support.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mideast mediator Oman at the center of a key first Iran-US meeting over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: MUSCAT, Oman (AP) — Once again, some of the highest stakes in Middle East geopolitics will be discussed in this quiet coastal city without skyscrapers.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In a turbulent time, Turkey lays claim to a role of stability for Europe, Syria and global trade
item.description: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan on Friday sought to position his country as a point of stability in an increasingly tumultuous world, saying Turkey was essential for European security and able to span geopolitical divisions over Ukraine, Syria and U.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Wilks, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Thousands of children have been raped and sexually abused in eastern Congo, UNICEF says
item.description: Thousands of children have been subjected to rape and sexual violence in the conflict-battered eastern Congo over the course of two months, the U.N. children’s agency said Friday, warning that existing funding gaps meant that hundreds of thousands were deprived of protection.
item.dc:creator: Monika Pronczuk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Greenland space base commander removed after Vance criticism
item.description: The Trump administration removed the Greenland space base commander Thursday after discovering the leader sent out an email breaking stride with the vice president.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: China's Xi tells Spanish PM that partnership needed, no mention of talks with US
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China calls on Western countries to work to support multilateralism and open cooperation, Chinese President Xi Jinping told Spain’s visiting Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez on Friday, as Beijing woos allies for its escalating tariff fight with U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: SUMAN NAISHADHAM and CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australian opposition leader says his home was the target of an alleged bomb plot
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australian opposition leader Peter Dutton confirmed on Friday that his family home had been the target of an alleged bomb plot, but said concerns for his personal safety did not restrict his election campaigning.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European countries vow billions in military support for Ukraine as US envoy meets Putin
item.description: European countries are vowing to sends billions of dollars in further funding to help Ukraine keep fighting Russia’s invasion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A US Navy captain ordered a military funeral for a kamikaze pilot during WWII. Here's why
item.description: PEARL HARBOR, Hawaii (AP) — A Japanese pilot slammed his Zero fighter plane into the USS Missouri and ignited a fireball on April 11, 1945, during the Battle of Okinawa. The suicide attack instantly killed the pilot, but none of the battleship's crew members were badly hurt.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea establishes diplomatic relations with Syria, a longtime friend of rival North Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea has established diplomatic relations with Syria’s new Islamist government, Seoul’s foreign ministry said Friday, months after a rebel coalition ousted President Bashar Assad, who had maintained close ties with North Korea.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel releases Palestinian who took part in attack at 13 and developed mental illness in prison
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel on Thursday released a Palestinian who took part in an attack when he was 13 and developed schizophrenia in prison as requests for early release were denied. Israel says Ahmad Manasra is a terrorist who tried to kill Jews his own age.
item.dc:creator: MAHMOUD ILLEAN and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel releases 10 Palestinians detained from Gaza. They say they suffered abuse
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Ten Palestinians detained from Gaza by Israeli troops were freed and returned to the territory on Thursday, saying they had suffered constant abuse while imprisoned.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine 'coalition of the willing' presses ahead with force planning amid doubts about US support
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Britain's defense minister on Thursday urged his counterparts from around 30 countries to press ahead with plans to deploy troops to Ukraine to police any future peace agreement with Russia, as questions remained over whether the United States would provide backup.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and SAM McNEIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrat says Trump-Xi in 'testosterone battle' with tariff war
item.description: Sen. Jeff Merkley (D-Ore.) on Thursday likened the U.S. tariff war with China to a “testosterone battle” between President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Yellen: Trump 'has taken a wrecking ball' to economy with trade war
item.description: Former Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen, who served under former President Biden, said President Trump “has taken a wrecking ball” to the economy.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: US ambassador to Ukraine stepping down
item.description: U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine Bridget Brink is stepping down from the role she held for nearly three years, according to the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: The United States and China are locked in a faceoff over tariffs. No one wants to blink first
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The tariff fight between the world's two largest economies spiraled into greater peril Wednesday as President Donald Trump tried to narrow his global trade war into a direct — and risky — faceoff with Beijing.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Influencer Andrew Tate accused of pointing gun at woman's face ahead of London civil case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — One of four women suing influencer and self-styled misogynist Andrew Tate for sexual assault said he whipped her with a belt and once stuck a gun in her face, according to court documents that have been filed in London.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tariffs on China are 145%, White House clarifies, as stocks slump
item.description: World markets skyrocketed Thursday in the wake of President Donald Trump's 90-day retaliatory tariff pause.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis meets privately with King Charles and Queen Camilla during his Vatican convalescence
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis met privately with King Charles III and Queen Camilla on Wednesday at the Vatican during the royal couple's four-day state visit to Italy and on the occasion of their 20th wedding anniversary, the Vatican announced.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, DANICA KIRKA and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Countries will follow Trump imposing tariffs on China: Gordon Chang
item.description: Chang, author of the new book "Plan Red: China's Project to Destroy America," said he expects to see tariffs rise on Chinese goods worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Conservative leader Friedrich Merz secures German government coalition after months of political drift
item.description: German's center-right Christian Democratic Union and center-left Social Democrats have reached a deal to form a new German government under Friedrich Merz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indonesia offers to temporarily shelter wounded and orphaned Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto said Wednesday that his country will offer temporary shelter to Palestinian medical evacuees and children orphaned by the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says 2 Chinese were caught fighting alongside Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian military has captured two Chinese men fighting alongside the Russian army in the eastern Donetsk region and has information that “significantly more” are with Russian forces, Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Florida men charged in 'truly heinous' global child porn ring involving over 1M files
item.description: The Florida Department of Law Enforcement arrest seven people in Florida for an international child pornography ring involving over one million files purchased through social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Slain Hamas hostage's family fights for the release of those still in Gaza
item.description: Oded Lifshitz, an 83-year-old great-grandfather, returned to Israel in a coffin more than 500 days after he was taken from his home. Now, his family is fighting for the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prince Harry appeals the loss of his UK security detail
item.description: Harry lost his government-funded protection in February 2020 after he stepped down from his role and moved to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: US admiral at NATO fired in expanding national security purge
item.description: U.S. Navy Vice Admiral Shoshana Chatfield is one of only a handful of female Navy three-star officers.
item.dc:creator: Phil Stewart, Idrees Ali, Lili Bayer / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Israel controls 50% of Gaza after razing land to expand its buffer zone
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel has dramatically expanded its footprint in the Gaza Strip since relaunching its war against Hamas last month. It now controls more than 50% of the territory and is squeezing Palestinians into shrinking wedges of land.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels killed at least six people, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine will send a team to the US next week for talks on a new draft mineral deal
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine will send a team to Washington next week to begin negotiations on a new draft of a deal that would give the U.S. access to Ukraine’s valuable mineral resources, Economy Minister Yuliia Svyrydenko told The Associated Press. “The new draft agreement from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope makes surprise appearance at St. Peter’s Square, 2 weeks after leaving hospital
item.description: Pope Francis made a surprise entrance to St. Peter’s Square during a special Jubilee Mass for the sick and health workers on Sunday, marking his first public appearance at the Vatican since leaving t he hospital two weeks ago after a life-threatening bout with pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Silvia Stellacci, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope appears in St. Peter's Square for first time in weeks
item.description: Pope Francis greeted crowds in St. Peter's Square on Sunday, his first public appearance since being discharged from the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials say a Russian strike in central Ukraine kills 14 people, including six children
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian ballistic missile strike Friday on a central Ukrainian city killed at least 14 people, including six children, Ukrainian officials said, as U.S. and European leaders pressed Russia to accept a ceasefire in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9
